description_long: This helicopter features a streamlined fuselage with a dark green and black color scheme, typical of utility or military helicopters such as the Bell UH-1 Huey. The main rotor consists of two long, slender blades with yellow tips, while the tail rotor is mounted on the left side of the tail boom. The cockpit is equipped with large, rectangular windows that provide wide visibility for the pilots, and the side doors and windows are also prominent. The helicopter rests on a pair of tubular metal landing skids, which are simple and functional, supporting the aircraft's weight on the ground. The tail boom extends straight back, ending in a vertical fin and a horizontal stabilizer, with the tail rotor attached. No visible registration numbers or markings are present on the exterior.
description: A dark green and black utility helicopter with large cockpit windows, two-blade main and tail rotors, and tubular landing skids.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide cockpit windows, nose, and the main rotor blades extending horizontally.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the full fuselage, cockpit, side windows, landing skids, and the main and tail rotors.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the horizontal and vertical stabilizers.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the opposite side of the fuselage, cockpit, and landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, narrow main rotor blades, likely made of composite or metal, with black coloring and yellow tips; blades appear straight and of substantial length, typical for utility helicopters.
attribute_tail_rotor: Two-blade tail rotor mounted on the left side of the tail boom, oriented vertically, with a simple design and no visible protective shroud; blades are black with possible yellow tips.
attribute_landing_skids: Tubular metal landing skids, painted black, with a simple, straight design and crossbars connecting the skids for structural support.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Four large, rectangular cockpit windows at the front, with two main panes and two smaller side panes; windows are clear with dark framing and provide wide visibility.
